props.message:
The Wake.

Carved into the cliffs where the sun first touches the realm. The boot-soldiers keep the morning.

An inscription cut into the rock:

"At the first pale, we stand. At the first breath, we sing. At the first light, the realm wakes — and we have not slept."

ORDER OF THE MORNING

1. The captain reads the names.
2. The bell sounds three times.
3. The sun is greeted at the eastern step.
4. The veterans are saluted.
5. The day is given to Lirien.

— By the hand of Captain Iren.

MEMORIAL SLAB

They kept the morning so we could live the day.

  Hesper of the long watch.
  Olber, who sang the third bell.
  Wenna, blade and breath.
  Vaella, who knew every name.
  Tova, last of the East-keep.

Walk well.

Carved deep into the cliff:

"These stones were old before the Wyrdmark had a name. They will be old when the name is forgotten. Stand among them, and stand straight."

NOTICE

New recruits report to Captain Iren at the eastern step before second bell. Bring nothing but breath. The Wake provides the rest.

A small stone, set apart:

"For the morning that almost did not come. — H."

Boot-soldier inscription:

"What we do, no one sees. If we do it well, no one ever will. By the Source, this is enough."

props.open_message: A drawer of river-pebbles, kept for the slings of recruits.
A heart-flower wrapped in linen — a recruit's first-day gift.
An iron key on a worn cord. Stamped with the Wake's mark.
A green-glass bottle. Inside: a small light that breathes.

root.speaker: Captain Iren
root.text: Stand straight, traveler. You are at the Wake. We hold the cliffs and we hold the morning. State your business briefly.

choices.label: Tell me of the morning ritual.

purpose.speaker: Captain Iren
purpose.text: The Wake is where the realm begins again, every morning. Without us — without the rite — the day would not catch. I do not say this lightly. I say it because it is so.

ritual.speaker: Captain Iren
ritual.text: The names are read. The bell is sounded thrice. The sun is greeted at the eastern step. Then the day is given to the realm. We have done it for as long as anyone has counted, and longer than that.

choices.label: What if a morning is missed?

missed.speaker: Captain Iren
missed.text: It will not be missed. Not while I stand. Not while there is one recruit left to read a name. — Ask the veteran, if you must hear more. He was here for the morning that nearly did not catch.

tired.speaker: Captain Iren
tired.text: I am... well-rested. Thank you for your concern. — *a pause* — The mornings are long. There is no other kind. By the Source, that is enough said.

serve.speaker: Captain Iren
serve.text: I serve the Wake. The Wake serves the morning. The morning serves — *she straightens, looks past you* — it serves the realm. That is the answer the rite gives. Take it as given.

serve_evade.speaker: Captain Iren
serve_evade.text: *she looks at the stone under her boots* The names of the served are not for the asking. We hold the morning. That has always been enough.

bye.speaker: Captain Iren
bye.text: Walk well. Mind the western step — something has come down off the cliffs that should not be there.

root.speaker: Captain Vael
root.text: I have been watching you cross the cliffs. Will you hear me out?

offer.speaker: Captain Vael
offer.text: You've broken three of the realm's locks. Take this — Glimblade. Forged for one who keeps showing up.

given.speaker: Captain Vael
given.text: It will keep its edge longer than my watch will. Use it well.

already.speaker: Captain Vael
already.text: Glimblade is yours. Wear it where the realm can see it.

not_yet.speaker: Captain Vael
not_yet.text: Come back when you've broken a third lock. Then we can speak of forged things.

bye.speaker: Captain Vael
bye.text: Walk well. The morning still holds.

root.speaker: Recruit Pol
root.text: Oh — hi! Hi. I'm Pol, I'm with the boot-soldiers now, did you know? I just joined three mornings ago and I have already heard the bell TWICE. Have you heard the morning ritual yet?

tellme.speaker: Recruit Pol
tellme.text: You have to hear it from inside the cliffs. That's the whole thing. The stones do something to the bell — it sounds like the cliffs are humming back at it. The captain says it's the Source answering. I think the captain is right but I don't tell her that.

shy.speaker: Recruit Pol
shy.text: Because she's the CAPTAIN. You don't just walk up and tell the captain she was right. You wait until she asks. I'm waiting. I'll be waiting a while.

told.speaker: Recruit Pol
told.text: Lirien's breath, isn't it something? My grandmother told me about it but she never said it would feel like the cliffs were singing at you. The first time, I forgot to breathe. The second time, I did better.

bye.speaker: Recruit Pol
bye.text: Walk well! Come back at dawn if you can. The cliffs are nice but they are amazing in the morning.

root.speaker: Veteran Hask
root.text: *he is looking past you, at the cliff face, or perhaps at nothing* — Mm. A traveler. Speak if you wish. I will hear you well enough.

wake.speaker: Veteran Hask
wake.text: *still not looking at you* The Wake holds the morning. Most days, the morning catches itself. Some days, the rite has to coax it. Most kin think it cannot fail. They think this because we have not let it fail.

served.speaker: Veteran Hask
served.text: Long enough that the captain was a recruit when I had already grown tired. The names on the slab — I knew most of them. I read the third bell at Olber's last morning. He was not a quiet man. The bell was loud enough.

worst.speaker: Veteran Hask
worst.text: *his voice is very even* There was a morning when the breath was thin and the Wake nearly didn't catch. The bell rang and the cliffs did not answer. We held the rite — we held it harder than we had ever held a thing — and after a long while the morning came. The realm runs on something fragile, traveler. We do not say so. But we know.

bye.speaker: Veteran Hask
bye.text: *he tilts his head, eyes still on the stone* Walk well. Mind the morning when next you cross it.

root.speaker: Tmux-keeper
root.text:
*she tends a row of small windows, each looking onto a different corner of the realm*

Detach and come back, walker. Your session waits. The keepers do not let it close.

choices.label: What if I forget the name?

fname.speaker: Tmux-keeper
fname.text: We call yours 'journey.' If you forget, ask. We will remember.
